name: QUO Inbound Source Proof
purpose: Bring QUO forward as the first Phone / Voice / Text sensory portal into the existing CRM -> Teams -> triage lane, while keeping all outbound phone/SMS behavior blocked.

dependsOn.b8bLiveHardening: Complete; B10a local work did not require it, but the live Journey loop is now hardened.
dependsOn.b9bSelectedTriage: Complete for one internal G0 read-only proof against CRM item #32; future B9 normal-client reads and G1 rows remain separately gated.

currentState.b10bMode: complete implementation-ready source contract/design pack; no QUO API, webhook, connector, secret read, CRM write, Teams post, or real phone traffic
currentState.b10c0Mode: complete local QUO API key readiness; key stored under ignored .local path and dry-run probe passed with no live API read
currentState.futureLiveProof: B10c.1 or later source-expansion stage after exact approval
currentState.triageLane: B2/B3/B4 New Signal triage and Suggested row workflow

authority.localSyntheticPayloadDesign: G0/R0 when using redacted local-only examples
authority.selectedNoRealClientProof: G2/R2 or G3/R3 depending on ingress surface and whether the proof creates a CRM row from an external webhook
authority.webhookOrConnectorSetup: G3/R3 because it introduces an external source, secret/signature handling, and active CRM ingress
authority.outboundSmsOrCallback: G3/R3 later decision, blocked in B10

sourcePrinciples: QUO is a sensory portal, not a separate CRM and not a separate phone bot.
CRM - New Signals remains the source of truth for new work.
Teams remains the internal attention surface.
The M365 Interaction Agent produces evidence and supervised suggestions only.
Freedom, Guided AI Labs Operating System, and Graphify can consume stable source ids later, but B10 does not couple directly to them.

ingressOptions.name: manualBridge
ingressOptions.governance: G0/G1 for local evidence, G2 if a CRM row is created manually
ingressOptions.summary: Adam keeps QUO notifications on the phone and manually records selected events into CRM.
ingressOptions.fit: Lowest risk and useful as a fallback, but it does not prove automated source ingress.

ingressOptions.name: noCodeWebhookBridge
ingressOptions.summary: QUO webhook sends to Make, Zapier, or Power Automate, then creates a CRM - New Signals row.
ingressOptions.fit: Fast proof if Adam accepts a temporary third-party automation/secrets surface.

ingressOptions.name: purposeBuiltIngressAdapter
ingressOptions.summary: A small quo-signal-ingress endpoint verifies signatures, normalizes payloads, and writes one CRM signal.
ingressOptions.fit: Best production shape when the webhook contract and secret/revoke path are known.

recommendedDefault.proofPath: Use B10a local readiness plus the B10b source contract first. For B10c or later live proof, prefer one selected no-real-client QUO event through a signed ingress path. Use manual bridge only as a fallback evidence step if webhook details are not ready.
recommendedDefault.businessNumbers: Only Adam-approved QUO number(s) count as business intake.

eventClasses.notes: Default to High until Adam defines a more nuanced response rule.
Do not store transcript text unless retention and privacy are approved.
Store a short preview in SourceText; raw message retention stays behind approval.
Create a new signal only when it represents new work; otherwise append/advisory is a later design.
Do not create CRM work by default; use as enrichment only after relationship rules exist.

SourceTextMetadata: Caller phone and known contact name when available

recommendedDefault.duplicatePolicy: Primary key is QUO sourceEventId. Fallback key is conversationId or callId plus normalized fromPhone and eventTimestamp bucket. One existing match returns/maps to the existing CRM item; zero matches creates one CRM item; more than one match stops for Adam review.
recommendedDefault.rawPayloadPolicy: Raw QUO payloads stay out of git and inventory by default. Store raw payload evidence only under .local/quo-ingress when approved. Commit only sanitized summaries, digests, mappings, and proof packets.
recommendedDefault.retentionDefault: Keep sanitized proof summaries in inventory. Keep raw local payloads only long enough to validate the proof, then redact or remove according to the B10 live approval.
recommendedDefault.disablePath: Every live bridge must have a named owner, exact webhook/flow/adapter location, and one-command or UI pause path before proof.

scope: Name the QUO business intake number(s) allowed for the proof.
Name the event class allowed for the first no-real-client proof.
Choose ingress option: manual bridge, no-code webhook bridge, or purpose-built ingress adapter.
Approve secret/signature verification storage and revoke path.
Approve raw payload evidence location and retention/redaction rule.
Approve duplicate/idempotency rule for the selected event class.
Approve disable/pause path and named owner.
Run one no-real-client or internal QUO event through CRM - New Signals, Teams alert, and G0 triage evidence.
stopConditions: No real customer call/SMS proof before synthetic or internal proof passes.
No webhook/API secret in git, docs, inventory, browser code, or DirectLink handoff.
No third-party automation without named owner and disable path.
No CRM merge, delete, suppression, task, reminder, or external message.
No QUO path that bypasses CRM - New Signals.

b10bPlaceholderAcceptance: Event taxonomy for missed call, voicemail, inbound SMS, completed call summary, and contact update is recorded.
Normalized CRM - New Signals shape is recorded with IntakeSource = QUO.
Duplicate/idempotency rule and fallback key strategy are recorded.
Raw payload retention/redaction policy is recorded without storing raw QUO payloads in git.
Visible approval surface, disable/revoke path, and named owner expectations are recorded.
Synthetic/no-real-client fixture expectations are recorded without requiring a real QUO payload.
No QUO API call, webhook, connector setup, secret read, CRM write, Teams post, external send, or real phone traffic occurs during B10b.
acceptance: B10a local packet exists and names event classes, ingress options, normalized CRM shape, duplicate rule, raw payload policy, disable path, evidence targets, stop conditions, and the future approval phrase.
Event mapping, live decision worksheet, and proof checklist CSVs exist.
B10b is closed as an implementation-ready source contract/design pack without live QUO API work.
Future B10c/later proof can be scoped to one no-real-client or internal event before any real customer traffic is used.
No QUO connector, webhook, API call, CRM write, Teams post, external send, or secret read occurs during B10a.
Outbound SMS/callback remains blocked until a later explicit G3/R3 decision.
